text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Tone Sandhi (Tonal Transformations)}} is defined as: - **3rd Tone Sandhi**: When two 3rd tones occur in succession, the first shifts to a 2nd tone (e.g., *nǐ hǎo* -> *ní hǎo* [ni˧˥ xaʊ˨˩˦]).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.
usage:
Rule: Tone Sandhi (Tonal Transformations)
Description: - **3rd Tone Sandhi**: When two 3rd tones occur in succession, the first shifts to a 2nd tone (e.g., *nǐ hǎo* -> *ní hǎo* [ni˧˥ xaʊ˨˩˦]).
spanish: En la fonética de Chinese, la regla de 'Tone Sandhi (Tonal Transformations)' es: - **3rd Tone Sandhi**: When two 3rd tones occur in succession, the first shifts to a 2nd tone (e.g., *nǐ hǎo* -> *ní hǎo* [ni˧˥ xaʊ˨˩˦]).

text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Bu Sandhi}} is defined as: *bù* (4th tone) shifts to 2nd tone *bú* when preceding another 4th tone (e.g., *bù qù* -> *bú qù*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.
usage:
Rule: Bu Sandhi
Description: *bù* (4th tone) shifts to 2nd tone *bú* when preceding another 4th tone (e.g., *bù qù* -> *bú qù*).
spanish: En la fonética de Chinese, la regla de 'Bu Sandhi' es: *bù* (4th tone) shifts to 2nd tone *bú* when preceding another 4th tone (e.g., *bù qù* -> *bú qù*).

text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Yi Sandhi}} is defined as: *yī* (1st tone) becomes 4th tone *yì* before 1st, 2nd, and 3rd tones (e.g., *yī tiān* -> *yì tiān*), and becomes 2nd tone *yí* before 4th tone (e.g., *yī yàng* -> *yí yàng*).
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.
usage:
Rule: Yi Sandhi
Description: *yī* (1st tone) becomes 4th tone *yì* before 1st, 2nd, and 3rd tones (e.g., *yī tiān* -> *yì tiān*), and becomes 2nd tone *yí* before 4th tone (e.g., *yī yàng* -> *yí yàng*).
spanish: En la fonética de Chinese, la regla de 'Yi Sandhi' es: *yī* (1st tone) becomes 4th tone *yì* before 1st, 2nd, and 3rd tones (e.g., *yī tiān* -> *yì tiān*), and becomes 2nd tone *yí* before 4th tone (e.g., *yī yàng* -> *yí yàng*).

text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Half 3rd Tone Rule}} is defined as: When a 3rd tone is followed by any tone other than another 3rd tone (i.e., 1st, 2nd, 4th, or neutral), it only dips down to low pitch and doesn't rise at the end. This is the most common realization of the 3rd tone in natural speech.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.
usage:
Rule: Half 3rd Tone Rule
Description: When a 3rd tone is followed by any tone other than another 3rd tone (i.e., 1st, 2nd, 4th, or neutral), it only dips down to low pitch and doesn't rise at the end. This is the most common realization of the 3rd tone in natural speech.
spanish: En la fonética de Chinese, la regla de 'Half 3rd Tone Rule' es: When a 3rd tone is followed by any tone other than another 3rd tone (i.e., 1st, 2nd, 4th, or neutral), it only dips down to low pitch and doesn't rise at the end. This is the most common realization of the 3rd tone in natural speech.

text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Tonal Identity}} is defined as: Tone is essential for meaning; changing the tone changes the word (e.g., *mā* "mother" vs *mǎ* "horse").
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.
usage:

Description: Tone is essential for meaning; changing the tone changes the word (e.g., *mā* "mother" vs *mǎ* "horse").

text: In Chinese phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Neutral Tone}} is defined as: Syllables in certain positions (like the second half of some compounds) lose their distinct tone and are pronounced short and light.
explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Chinese.

text: What is the Chinese pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The "Zhiao" Shortcut}}? In rapid conversational Mandarin, "wǒ bù zhī dào" (I don't know) is contracted to "wǒ bù zhiao". Practice blending the 'zh' and 'd' into a single fast 'j/zh' slide!
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for Chinese.
usage:
Tip: The "Zhiao" Shortcut
In rapid conversational Mandarin, "wǒ bù zhī dào" (I don't know) is contracted to "wǒ bù zhiao". Practice blending the 'zh' and 'd' into a single fast 'j/zh' slide!
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de Chinese para 'The "Zhiao" Shortcut'? In rapid conversational Mandarin, "wǒ bù zhī dào" (I don't know) is contracted to "wǒ bù zhiao". Practice blending the 'zh' and 'd' into a single fast 'j/zh' slide!

text: What is the Chinese pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Half 3rd Tone Rule}}? To sound natural, do not try to make the full dipping-rising sound for every 3rd tone. In 90% of cases (like in "lǎo shī" or "lǎo bǎn"), just make a low, flat chest tone and immediately jump to the next syllable.
explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for Chinese.
usage:
Tip: The Half 3rd Tone Rule
To sound natural, do not try to make the full dipping-rising sound for every 3rd tone. In 90% of cases (like in "lǎo shī" or "lǎo bǎn"), just make a low, flat chest tone and immediately jump to the next syllable.
spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de Chinese para 'The Half 3rd Tone Rule'? To sound natural, do not try to make the full dipping-rising sound for every 3rd tone. In 90% of cases (like in "lǎo shī" or "lǎo bǎn"), just make a low, flat chest tone and immediately jump to the next syllable.

text: I would like to check in this suitcase, and I have one {{c1::carry-on bag}} to bring into the cabin.
explanation: A carry-on bag (or hand luggage) is the small suitcase or backpack that passengers are permitted to bring onto the plane with them rather than checking it.
usage:
Pattern: carry-on bag / carry-on luggage
  • Make sure your carry-on bag fits in the overhead bin.
  • I only travel with a carry-on bag to save time.
spanish: Me gustaría registrar esta maleta, y tengo una maleta de mano para llevar en la cabina.

scenario: English Phonetics: Airport Gate Connected Speech ✈️
text: At the gate, the announcement "We will begin boarding in ten minutes" blends the last two words via consonant-to-consonant linking, making "in ten" sound like {{c1::"int-ten" [ɪnˈtɛn]}}.
explanation: When a word ends in /n/ and the next begins with /t/, the alveolar nasal /n/ assimilates or links directly to the alveolar stop /t/ to allow smooth, rapid articulation, resulting in a single elongated nasal-to-stop transition.
usage:
Phonetic Drill: in ten minutes → "in-ten" [ɪnˈtɛn]
Contrastive Practice: Pronounce both 'n' and 't' as a single continuous block without a glottal pause.
spanish: En la puerta, el anuncio "We will begin boarding in ten minutes" fusiona las últimas dos palabras mediante enlace consonante-consonante, haciendo que "in ten" suene como "int-ten".

scenario: English Daily Life: Airport Baggage Claim ✈️
text: At the baggage claim desk, if your luggage is missing, you should report it politely saying: "Excuse me, my bag {{c1::didn't come out}} on the carousel, and I'd like to {{c2::file a claim}}."
explanation: To "file a claim" is the official terminology for registering a lost item report. Saying "didn't come out" is the natural phrasal expression for baggage that did not arrive.
usage:
Pattern: file a claim / didn't come out on [carousel]
  • I need to file a claim for my damaged baggage.
  • My suitcase didn't come out on carousel 4.
spanish: En el mostrador de reclamo de equipaje, si tu maleta se perdió, debes reportarlo diciendo educadamente: "Excuse me, my bag didn't come out on the carousel, and I'd like to file a claim."

text: Could you please tell me where the {{c1::baggage drop-off}} counter is?
explanation: The 'baggage drop-off' is the designated counter where passengers who have already checked in online can drop off their luggage before heading to security.
usage:
Pattern: baggage drop-off / bag drop
  • Where is the bag drop for airline passengers?
  • You can bypass the main line if you only need the baggage drop-off.
spanish: ¿Podría decirme por favor dónde está el mostrador de entrega de equipaje?

text: I need to check in my luggage, but I want to keep this briefcase as my {{c1::personal item}}.
explanation: A 'personal item' is a small bag (like a purse, laptop bag, or small backpack) that must fit under the seat in front of you on a plane, distinct from standard carry-on luggage.
usage:
Pattern: personal item
  • Most budget airlines allow one personal item for free.
  • Your laptop bag counts as a personal item.
spanish: Necesito registrar mi equipaje, pero quiero conservar este maletín como mi artículo personal.

text: The flight has been delayed, so we need to wait at the {{c1::departure gate}}.
explanation: The 'departure gate' (or boarding gate) is the specific area in an airport terminal where passengers assemble to board their aircraft.
usage:
Pattern: departure gate / boarding gate
  • Our departure gate has changed from gate 12 to gate 15.
  • Please report to the departure gate immediately.
spanish: El vuelo se ha retrasado, así que tenemos que esperar en la puerta de salida/embarque.

text: Excuse me, does this flight have a {{c1::layover}} in New York?
explanation: A 'layover' (or connection) is a period of waiting between flights at an intermediate airport before reaching the final destination.
usage:
Pattern: layover / connection
  • We have a three-hour layover in Chicago.
  • I prefer direct flights because I hate long layovers.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿este vuelo tiene una escala en Nueva York?

text: We need to go through {{c1::security screening}} before we can access the duty-free area.
explanation: The 'security screening' is the checkpoint in an airport where passengers and their baggage are inspected by security officers and scanning machines.
usage:
Pattern: security screening / security check
  • The line at the security screening was incredibly long.
  • Remove all laptops and liquids before security screening.
spanish: Necesitamos pasar por el control de seguridad antes de poder acceder al área libre de impuestos.

text: Please have your {{c1::boarding pass}} and ID ready for inspection.
explanation: A 'boarding pass' is a document provided by an airline during check-in, giving a passenger permission to board the airplane for a specific flight.
usage:
Pattern: boarding pass / boarding document
  • You can show your digital boarding pass on your phone.
  • Please print your boarding pass at the kiosk.
spanish: Por favor, tenga su pase de abordar e identificación listos para inspección.

text: My flight was overbooked, and they offered me travel vouchers if I {{c1::volunteered to take}} a later flight.
explanation: To 'volunteer to take' a later flight means agreeing to give up your seat on an overbooked flight in exchange for compensation (such as travel vouchers or money).
usage:
Pattern: volunteer to take [flight]
  • No one volunteered to take the next flight, so they started bumped passengers.
  • I volunteered to take a later flight because the vouchers were worth $500.
spanish: Mi vuelo estaba sobrevendido, y me ofrecieron vales de viaje si me ofrecía como voluntario para tomar un vuelo posterior.

text: Excuse me, could you tell me how to {{c1::get to}} the nearest train station?
explanation: get to is the most common conversational phrasal verb meaning to reach or arrive at a destination.
usage:
Pattern: get to [place]
  • How do I get to the museum from here?
  • It takes 20 minutes to get to the office.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿podría decirme cómo llegar a la estación de tren más cercana?

text: Go straight ahead for two blocks, then {{c1::take a left}} at the traffic light.
explanation: To 'take a left' is a very common idiomatic phrase meaning to turn left at a specific intersection or landmark.
usage:
Pattern: take a left / make a left / turn left
  • Take a left at the next street.
  • You will need to take a left after you pass the school.
spanish: Siga derecho por dos cuadras, luego doble a la izquierda en el semáforo.

text: Excuse me, is there a pharmacy {{c1::within walking distance}} of here?
explanation: 'Within walking distance' means close enough to be reached easily by walking (typically less than 10-15 minutes away).
usage:
Pattern: within walking distance [of something]
  • The hotel is within walking distance of the beach.
  • We live within walking distance of the office.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿hay alguna farmacia a corta distancia a pie de aquí?

text: You can't miss it; it's right {{c1::across from}} the post office.
explanation: 'Across from' means directly opposite or on the other side of a street, hallway, or space.
usage:
Pattern: across from [place] / opposite
  • The cafe is across from the library.
  • He sat across from me during the meeting.
spanish: No tiene pérdida; está justo enfrente de la oficina de correos.

text: To get to the museum, you need to {{c1::cross the street}} and walk past the bank.
explanation: To 'cross the street' means to walk from one side of a road to the opposite side.
usage:
Pattern: cross the street / cross the road
  • Look both ways before you cross the street.
  • We crossed the street to avoid the construction work.
spanish: Para llegar al museo, debe cruzar la calle y pasar el banco.

text: Excuse me, could you {{c1::point me in the direction of}} the nearest subway station?
explanation: To 'point someone in the direction of' means to show or tell someone the general path or heading to reach a destination.
usage:
Pattern: point [someone] in the direction of [place]
  • Can you point me in the direction of the restrooms?
  • She pointed us in the direction of the highway entrance.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿podría indicarme la dirección hacia la estación de metro más cercana?

text: The stadium is located on the {{c1::outer edge}} of the city.
explanation: The 'outer edge' refers to the boundary, periphery, or outskirts of an area, such as a city limit.
usage:
Pattern: outer edge / outskirts
  • They built a new retail park on the outer edge of town.
  • We live on the outer edge of the metropolitan area.
spanish: El estadio está ubicado en las afueras/extremo exterior de la ciudad.

text: If you hit the gas station, you've {{c1::gone too far}}; turn back.
explanation: To 'go too far' means to travel past the intended destination or target point.
usage:
Pattern: go too far / pass the destination
  • If you reach the river, you've gone too far.
  • I think we went too far; we should turn around.
spanish: Si llega a la gasolinera, se ha pasado; regrese.

text: Take the second exit at the {{c1::roundabout}} to stay on Main Street.
explanation: A 'roundabout' (or traffic circle) is a circular intersection where drivers travel counterclockwise (or clockwise in UK) around a central island.
usage:
Pattern: roundabout / traffic circle
  • Go straight through the first roundabout.
  • Turn right at the roundabout.
spanish: Tome la segunda salida en la rotonda para permanecer en la calle principal.

text: Is it quicker to walk or to {{c1::hail a cab}} from here?
explanation: To 'hail a cab' (or hail a taxi) means to signal a taxi on the street (typically by raising your arm) to stop and pick you up.
usage:
Pattern: hail a cab / catch a taxi
  • We stood in the rain trying to hail a cab for fifteen minutes.
  • It is easy to hail a cab in this neighborhood.
spanish: ¿Es más rápido caminar o pedir/hacerle señas a un taxi desde aquí?

text: Could I get a medium latte {{c1::to go}}, please?
explanation: to go is used when you want to take your food or drink out of the shop instead of consuming it there.
usage:
Pattern: Could I get a [size] [drink] to go, please?
  • Could I get a large Americano to go, please?
  • Could I get a small espresso to go, please?
spanish: ¿Me da un latte mediano para llevar, por favor?

text: I'd like to {{c1::substitute}} whole milk {{c1::with}} oat milk, please.
explanation: substitute A with B means to use B instead of A. This is the standard way to customize food or drink orders.
usage:
Pattern: substitute [original] with [alternative]
  • Can I substitute white bread with wheat bread?
  • I'd like to substitute fries with a side salad, please.
spanish: Me gustaría cambiar la leche entera por leche de avena, por favor.

text: Do you have any {{c1::sugar-free}} syrup options?
explanation: sugar-free means containing no sugar. Syrups are sweet liquid flavorings added to coffee (like vanilla or caramel).
usage:
Pattern: [adjective]-free (e.g., gluten-free, caffeine-free, tax-free)
  • Is this cookie gluten-free?
  • I'd prefer decaf, do you have caffeine-free coffee?
spanish: ¿Tienen opciones de jarabe sin azúcar?

text: Can I {{c1::pay by card}}?
explanation: pay by card means to pay using a credit or debit card rather than cash.
usage:
Pattern: pay by [method] (e.g., pay by card, pay by phone, pay by check) but pay in cash
  • You can pay by phone using Apple Pay.
  • Is it cheaper if I pay in cash?
spanish: ¿Puedo pagar con tarjeta?

text: I would like to order a large cold brew with a {{c1::splash of}} oat milk.
explanation: A 'splash of' is an informal measurement meaning a very small amount of liquid (typically milk, cream, or water) added to a drink.
usage:
Pattern: splash of [liquid]
  • Just a splash of milk in my tea, please.
  • He likes his whiskey with a splash of soda.
spanish: Me gustaría pedir un café extraído en frío grande con un chorrito de leche de avena.

text: Do you have any {{c1::dairy-free}} options for the latte?
explanation: 'Dairy-free' refers to foods or beverages that contain no milk or milk derivatives, suitable for lactose-intolerant or vegan customers.
usage:
Pattern: dairy-free milk / plant-based milk
  • Soy and almond milk are common dairy-free alternatives.
  • Is this caramel syrup dairy-free?
spanish: ¿Tienen alguna opción sin lácteos para el café con leche?

text: I'll have a double espresso, and can I get that {{c1::sweetened with}} honey?
explanation: To get something 'sweetened with' means to add a specific sweetener (like honey, sugar, syrup, or stevia) to a drink or food.
usage:
Pattern: sweetened with [sweetener]
  • I prefer my tea sweetened with raw sugar.
  • This lemonade is sweetened with agave nectar.
spanish: Tomaré un espresso doble, ¿y me lo pueden dar endulzado con miel?

text: Is there a password for the {{c1::guest Wi-Fi}} here?
explanation: 'Guest Wi-Fi' is a secondary wireless network provided by businesses for clients and visitors, keeping the internal network secure.
usage:
Pattern: guest Wi-Fi / public network
  • The guest Wi-Fi is free for customers who buy a drink.
  • Ask the barista for the guest Wi-Fi password.
spanish: ¿Hay contraseña para el Wi-Fi para invitados aquí?

text: I'd like to buy a bag of whole bean coffee, could you {{c1::grind it for}} drip?
explanation: To 'grind it for [method]' means to crush whole coffee beans into the specific particle size required for a brewing method (e.g. drip, espresso, french press).
usage:
Pattern: grind [coffee] for [brewing method]
  • Could you grind these beans for a French press?
  • I grind my own coffee beans for espresso every morning.
spanish: Me gustaría comprar una bolsa de café en grano entero, ¿podrían molerlo para cafetera de filtro?

text: Can I get a pastry to go, and please {{c1::warm it up}} for me?
explanation: To 'warm up' something means to make it warm or hot, usually by putting it in a microwave, oven, or toaster.
usage:
Pattern: warm [something] up / heat up
  • Would you like me to warm up your croissant?
  • I need to warm up my lunch in the microwave.
spanish: ¿Me da una factura/pan dulce para llevar, y por favor me la calienta?

text: Explaining to the landlord: "The basement has accumulated {{c1::mold and mildew}} due to the high {{c2::moisture levels}}, so we need a dehumidifier."
explanation: Mold and mildew refer to fungal growths caused by dampness. **Moisture** refers to wetness or condensation in the air.
usage:
Pattern: accumulate mold due to high moisture / run a dehumidifier
  • High moisture levels in the bathroom will cause mold and mildew.
  • We bought a dehumidifier to keep the basement dry.
spanish: Explicándole al propietario: "El sótano ha acumulado moho y humedad debido a los altos niveles de humedad, por lo que necesitamos un deshumidificador".

text: Troubleshooting the setup: "The smart lock has a {{c1::sync issue}} with the hub, so the door sensor doesn't {{c2::trigger the security routine}}."
explanation: Sync issue refers to synchronization failure. **Trigger a routine** means to automatically start a sequence of pre-programmed actions.
usage:
Pattern: have a sync issue / trigger a routine based on [event]
  • My phone is having a sync issue with the server.
  • Connecting the sensor will trigger the alarm routine.
spanish: Resolviendo problemas de la configuración: "La cerradura inteligente tiene un problema de sincronización con el concentrador, por lo que el sensor de la puerta no activa la rutina de seguridad".

text: Discussing the rent: "We need to pay the first month's utilities and the {{c1::security deposit}} before we can inspect the {{c2::floor plan}}."
explanation: Security deposit is a sum of money paid in advance to protect the landlord against damage. **Floor plan** is the scale diagram showing room layouts.
usage:
Pattern: pay a security deposit / check the floor plan
  • The landlord returned our security deposit when we moved out.
  • Let's study the floor plan to decide where to put the sofa.
spanish: Discutiendo el alquiler: "Debemos pagar los servicios del primer mes y el depósito de seguridad antes de poder inspeccionar el plano de distribución de la casa".

text: Instructing the barista: "If your {{c1::grind size}} is too coarse, it causes {{c2::channeling}}, resulting in a watery crema."
explanation: Grind size determines the speed of water flow. **Channeling** occurs when water finds paths of least resistance through the coffee puck instead of extracting evenly.
usage:
Pattern: adjust the grind size / prevent channeling during extraction
  • If the grind size is too fine, the espresso will extract too slowly.
  • Tamping the coffee evenly prevents channeling.
spanish: Instruyendo al barista: "Si el tamaño de la molienda es demasiado grueso, causa canalización, lo que resulta en una crema aguada".

text: Explaining the game: "This is a {{c1::turn-based game}} with specific card mechanics, including a roll of dice to resolve any {{c2::tie-breaker}}."
explanation: Turn-based denotes a gameplay mechanic where players take turns. **Tie-breaker** is the rule or action that determines a winner when scores are equal.
usage:
Pattern: turn-based game / resolve a tie-breaker
  • Civilization is a famous turn-based strategy game.
  • The team won the match in a sudden-death tie-breaker.
spanish: Explicando el juego: "Este es un juego por turnos con mecánicas de cartas específicas, que incluye un lanzamiento de dados para resolver cualquier desempate".

text: Don't forget to {{c1::take out the trash}} before the garbage truck arrives tomorrow morning.
explanation: To 'take out the trash' means to carry refuse or garbage containers from inside the house to the outdoor collection spot.
usage:
Pattern: take out the trash / take out the rubbish
  • It is your turn to take out the trash tonight.
  • I took out the trash before going to bed.
spanish: No olvides sacar la basura antes de que llegue el camión de la basura mañana por la mañana.

text: I need to {{c1::run some errands}}, including dropping off the dry cleaning and buying groceries.
explanation: To 'run errands' means to take short trips to complete daily tasks or chores outside the house (e.g. post office, grocery store, cleaners).
usage:
Pattern: run errands / do errands
  • I have to run some errands this afternoon, so I'll be out of the office.
  • She spent the whole Saturday running errands.
spanish: Necesito hacer algunos mandados/recados, incluyendo dejar la ropa en la tintorería y comprar víveres.

text: The kitchen sink is leaking, so I need to {{c1::call a plumber}} to fix it.
explanation: To 'call a plumber' is a standard collocation for contacting a professional who installs and repairs pipes, fixtures, and water systems.
usage:
Pattern: call a plumber / hire a plumber
  • We had to call a plumber to unclog the drain.
  • The plumber charged $100 just to inspect the pipes.
spanish: El fregadero de la cocina gotea, así que necesito llamar a un plomero para que lo arregle.

text: We need to {{c1::stock up on}} toilet paper and paper towels this weekend.
explanation: To 'stock up on' means to buy a large quantity of something (usually household supplies or food) to keep for future use.
usage:
Pattern: stock up on [goods]
  • People stocked up on water and canned food before the storm.
  • I always stock up on coffee when it goes on sale.
spanish: Necesitamos abastecernos de papel higiénico y toallas de papel este fin de semana.

text: Could you please {{c1::put away the groceries}} in the pantry?
explanation: To 'put away groceries' means to place purchased food items in their proper storage locations (fridge, freezer, cabinets, pantry) after shopping.
usage:
Pattern: put away the groceries
  • Help me put away the groceries before the ice cream melts.
  • She was busy putting away the groceries.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor guardar las compras en la despensa?

text: I have a reservation under the name Perez, and I'd like to check if my room is {{c1::ready for check-in}}.
explanation: ready for check-in means that the hotel room has been cleaned, inspected, and is fully prepared for the guest to enter and receive their key.
usage:
Pattern: ready for check-in
  • Is my room ready for check-in? Standard check-in is at 3 PM.
  • The front desk agent told me the room is not ready yet.
spanish: Tengo una reservación a nombre de Pérez, y me gustaría verificar si mi habitación está lista para registrarme.

text: I have a reservation under the name Colin, and I'd like to {{c1::check in}}.
explanation: To 'check in' at a hotel is the formal process of registering, showing ID, providing payment, and receiving room keys.
usage:
Pattern: check in / check-in desk
  • We can't check in until 3:00 PM.
  • Where is the check-in counter?
spanish: Tengo una reservación bajo el nombre Colin, y me gustaría registrarme.

text: Does the room rate {{c1::include breakfast}}, or is there an extra charge?
explanation: To 'include breakfast' means that breakfast is provided for free as part of the price paid for the hotel room (often called 'complimentary breakfast').
usage:
Pattern: include breakfast / complimentary breakfast
  • Does this booking include breakfast?
  • Our stay includes a free buffet breakfast.
spanish: ¿La tarifa de la habitación incluye desayuno, o hay un cargo extra?

text: Could we request a {{c1::late check-out}} for tomorrow at 2:00 PM?
explanation: A 'late check-out' is an arrangement allowing hotel guests to stay in their room past the standard checkout time (typically 11:00 AM or 12:00 PM).
usage:
Pattern: late check-out / request a late check-out
  • We requested a late check-out because our flight is at night.
  • The hotel charged us a fee for late check-out.
spanish: ¿Podríamos solicitar una salida tardía para mañana a las 2:00 PM?

text: Excuse me, does the hotel offer a {{c1::shuttle service}} to the airport?
explanation: A 'shuttle service' is a regular, complimentary transport service (usually a van or bus) provided by a hotel to move guests to and from specific locations like airports or tourist sights.
usage:
Pattern: shuttle service / airport shuttle
  • The hotel shuttle service runs every half hour.
  • We took the free shuttle service to the beach.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿el hotel ofrece servicio de transporte gratuito al aeropuerto?

text: My key card is not working; could you please {{c1::re-key it}} for me?
explanation: To 're-key' a card means to reprogram the electronic magnetic strip or RFID chip on a hotel key card so that it opens the guest's room door.
usage:
Pattern: re-key the card / reprogram the key
  • My card got deactivated near my phone, so they had to re-key it.
  • The receptionist re-keyed my card in seconds.
spanish: Mi tarjeta llave no funciona; ¿podría volver a programarla para mí?

text: Could you please send some extra towels and toiletries up to {{c1::our room}}?
explanation: To 'send up to a room' is the standard phrasal expression used by guests when requesting room service or housekeeping to deliver items directly to their hotel room.
usage:
Pattern: send [items] up to our room
  • Could you send a bottle of water up to room 405?
  • Housekeeping will send the pillows up to your room.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviar algunas toallas y artículos de tocador adicionales a nuestra habitación?

text: Is there a {{c1::safe deposit box}} in the room to store our valuables?
explanation: A 'safe deposit box' (or in-room safe) is a small, secure electronic metal box inside a hotel room closet where guests can store passports, cash, and jewelry.
usage:
Pattern: safe deposit box / room safe
  • I put my passport in the safe deposit box.
  • Make sure you lock the safe deposit box before leaving.
spanish: ¿Hay caja fuerte en la habitación para guardar nuestros objetos de valor?

text: I need to make a phone call, is there free {{c1::local calling}} from the room?
explanation: 'Local calling' refers to telephone calls made to numbers within the same city or local calling area, which hotels often offer for free.
usage:
Pattern: local calling / long-distance calls
  • Local calling is free, but long-distance calls will be charged to your room.
  • Does this phone support free local calling?
spanish: Necesito hacer una llamada telefónica, ¿hay llamadas locales gratuitas desde la habitación?

text: We'd like to {{c1::leave our bags}} at the front desk until our room is ready.
explanation: To 'leave bags' (or store luggage) means to temporarily place your suitcases in the hotel's secure holding room when arriving before check-in or departing after check-out.
usage:
Pattern: leave our bags / store our luggage
  • Can we leave our bags here while we explore the city?
  • You can leave your bags with the bellhop.
spanish: Nos gustaría dejar nuestras maletas en recepción hasta que nuestra habitación esté lista.

text: Can I get a cheeseburger and {{c1::a side of}} fries, please?
explanation: a side of is the standard, natural collocation to order an additional side portion of food alongside your main dish.
usage:
Pattern: a side of [food]
  • I'd like a steak with a side of mashed potatoes.
  • Can I get a side of ranch dressing?
spanish: ¿Me da una hamburguesa con queso y una porción de papas fritas, por favor?

text: We are ready to order: I'll have the steak, and can I get the dressing {{c1::on the side}}?
explanation: To get a sauce or dressing 'on the side' means it should be served in a small separate cup rather than poured directly over the food.
usage:
Pattern: on the side / sauce on the side
  • I'd like a salad with vinaigrette on the side.
  • Can I get the gravy on the side?
spanish: Estamos listos para pedir: Tomaré el filete, ¿y me puede dar el aderezo aparte/a un lado?

text: Excuse me, I'm allergic to nuts; does this dish {{c1::contain any nuts}}?
explanation: To 'contain nuts' is a crucial health expression used to check if tree nuts or peanuts are used in the ingredients or preparation of a food item.
usage:
Pattern: contain nuts / nut-free
  • Does the pesto sauce contain any nuts?
  • Please let the chef know that my food must not contain any nuts.
spanish: Disculpe, soy alérgico a las nueces; ¿este plato contiene nueces?

text: Can we get a {{c1::pitcher of tap water}} for the table, please?
explanation: A 'pitcher of tap water' is a large container of municipal drinking water provided for free in US/UK restaurants, as opposed to bottled water.
usage:
Pattern: pitcher of tap water / jug of water
  • Just a pitcher of tap water is fine, thank you.
  • We always order a pitcher of tap water.
spanish: ¿Nos puede traer una jarra de agua del grifo para la mesa, por favor?

text: I'd like my steak cooked {{c1::medium-rare}}, please.
explanation: 'Medium-rare' is the standard cooking level for meat where the center is warm and red, generally preferred by culinary experts for texture and flavor.
usage:
Pattern: medium-rare / rare / medium / well-done
  • I prefer my ribeye cooked medium-rare.
  • He ordered his burger well-done.
spanish: Me gustaría mi filete término medio (tres cuartos), por favor.

text: Excuse me, we've been waiting for our food for thirty minutes; could you {{c1::check on our order}}?
explanation: To 'check on an order' is a polite way to ask a server to go to the kitchen and find out why the food is delayed.
usage:
Pattern: check on our order / verify the status
  • Could you please check on our order? It's taking a bit long.
  • I'll go to the kitchen to check on your order right away.
spanish: Disculpe, hemos estado esperando nuestra comida por treinta minutos; ¿podría revisar nuestro pedido?

text: This isn't what I ordered; I asked for the grilled chicken, not the {{c1::fried chicken}}.
explanation: This is a polite yet direct way to notify a server that they have brought the wrong dish to your table.
usage:
Pattern: this isn't what I ordered / wrong dish
  • Excuse me, this isn't what I ordered; I wanted a salad.
  • I apologize, I'll take this back and bring you the correct plate.
spanish: Esto no es lo que pedí; pedí el pollo a la parrilla, no el pollo frito.

text: We are done eating, could you please {{c1::bring us the check}}?
explanation: To 'bring the check' (or bring the bill) is the standard request to pay for the meal at the end of dining.
usage:
Pattern: bring us the check / can we get the bill
  • Excuse me, can you bring us the check when you have a moment?
  • He asked the waiter to bring the check.
spanish: Hemos terminado de comer, ¿podría por favor traernos la cuenta?

text: Do you have any vegan options, or can this dish be {{c1::made vegan}}?
explanation: To make a dish 'vegan' means modifying the recipe to exclude all animal products (meat, dairy, eggs, honey).
usage:
Pattern: made vegan / vegan-friendly
  • Can this pasta be made vegan if you remove the cheese?
  • Is there a vegan alternative for this sauce?
spanish: ¿Tienen alguna opción vegana, o este plato se puede hacer vegano?

text: Can we split the bill {{c1::evenly between}} the three of us?
explanation: To 'split the bill evenly' means to divide the total cost of a restaurant meal into equal portions among the diners.
usage:
Pattern: split the bill / go Dutch
  • Let's split the bill evenly to keep things simple.
  • We decided to split the bill instead of paying separately.
spanish: ¿Podemos dividir la cuenta en partes iguales entre los tres?

text: Where can I find the {{c1::dairy aisle}}?
explanation: An aisle is a passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket. dairy refers to milk-based products (cheese, yogurt, butter).
usage:
Pattern: [category] aisle (e.g., baking aisle, produce aisle, frozen food aisle)
  • The sugar is in the baking aisle.
  • Where is the produce aisle? I need some fresh apples.
spanish: ¿Dónde puedo encontrar el pasillo de lácteos?

text: Are these apples {{c1::on sale}} this week?
explanation: on sale means available at a reduced price (discounted). *Do not confuse with 'for sale', which just means available to buy.
usage:
Pattern: on sale (discounted) vs for sale (available to buy)
  • These shoes are on sale for 20% off.
  • Is that classic car for sale or is it just on display?
spanish: ¿Están estas manzanas en oferta esta semana?

text: Is there a limit on how many items I can bring to the {{c1::express lane}}?
explanation: An express lane is a checkout counter reserved for customers buying only a few items (e.g. 10 or less) to speed up service.
usage:
Pattern: express lane (typically labeled '10 items or less' or '15 items or fewer')
  • I only have 3 items, so I will use the express lane.
  • You can't go there; the express lane is for quick checkouts.
spanish: ¿Hay un límite de cuántos artículos puedo llevar a la caja rápida?

text: At the customer service counter, the customer requested: "I'd like a {{c1::full refund}} for this tablet because it has a {{c2::faulty charging port}}."
explanation: Refund is the return of payment. **Faulty** means defective or not working correctly.
usage:
Pattern: request a refund for [item] / faulty [part]
  • I need a full refund because the screen is cracked.
  • This laptop has a faulty battery that doesn't charge.
spanish: En el mostrador de servicio al cliente, el cliente solicitó: "Me gustaría un reembolso completo por esta tableta porque tiene un puerto de carga defectuoso".

text: At the flea market, he bargained: "Is that your {{c1::best price}}? If you {{c2::throw in the vintage watch}}, we have a deal."
explanation: Best price is the lowest price a seller is willing to accept. **Throw in** is a phrasal verb meaning to include something extra for free.
usage:
Pattern: throw in [item] / have a deal
  • If you throw in free shipping, I'll buy it.
  • We have a deal if you lower the price by ten dollars.
spanish: En el mercado de pulgas, regateó: "¿Es ese tu mejor precio? Si incluyes el reloj vintage, tenemos un trato".

text: When comparing laptops, he analyzed: "There are clear {{c1::trade-offs}} in portability, but the developer version gives you the most {{c2::bang for the buck}}."
explanation: Trade-offs are compromises made when choosing between two conflicting features. **Bang for the buck** is an idiom meaning value for money spent.
usage:
Pattern: trade-offs between [feature] and [feature] / get the most bang for the buck
  • There are always trade-offs between battery life and processing power.
  • This budget phone offers the best bang for the buck.
spanish: Al comparar computadoras portátiles, analizó: "Hay compensaciones claras en la portabilidad, pero la versión para desarrolladores te ofrece la mejor relación calidad-precio".

text: On the phone with the bank, she stated: "I need to {{c1::dispute a double charge}} that appeared on my recent {{c2::billing cycle statement}}."
explanation: Dispute a charge is the formal request to investigate an incorrect transaction. **Billing cycle statement** is the monthly bank report.
usage:
Pattern: dispute a charge on [account/statement]
  • You should dispute the charge immediately with your credit card company.
  • I check my billing cycle statement every month for errors.
spanish: Por teléfono con el banco, ella declaró: "Necesito impugnar un cargo doble que apareció en el estado de cuenta de mi ciclo de facturación reciente".

text: Before subscribing, he asked: "Is there an {{c1::auto-renewal policy}}? I want to make sure I read the {{c2::fine print}} to avoid hidden fees."
explanation: Auto-renewal is the automatic extension of a subscription. **Fine print** refers to the small text in a contract containing crucial restrictions and rules.
usage:
Pattern: auto-renewal policy / read the fine print
  • Always read the fine print before signing a software license.
  • The auto-renewal policy can be turned off in your account settings.
spanish: Antes de suscribirse, preguntó: "¿Existe una política de renovación automática? Quiero asegurarme de leer la letra pequeña para evitar cargos ocultos".

text: Excuse me, in which {{c1::aisle}} can I find the olive oil?
explanation: An 'aisle' is a long, narrow passage between shelves of goods in a supermarket or grocery store (the 's' is silent, pronounced /aɪl/).
usage:
Pattern: aisle [number] / product aisle
  • The baking ingredients are in aisle 4.
  • Excuse me, which aisle is the cereal in?
spanish: Disculpe, ¿en qué pasillo puedo encontrar el aceite de oliva?

text: I'll pay in cash, and I don't need a {{c1::receipt}}, thank you.
explanation: A 'receipt' is a printed slip of paper given to customers to prove that a financial transaction took place (the 'p' is silent, pronounced /rɪˈsiːt/).
usage:
Pattern: sales receipt / cash receipt
  • Always keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.
  • Do you want your receipt in the bag?
spanish: Pagaré en efectivo, y no necesito recibo, gracias.

text: I appreciate your perspective, so let's find a {{c1::middle ground}} that addresses both of our concerns.
explanation: middle ground is a common idiom meaning a compromise or a set of mutual concessions between two opposing viewpoints.
usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / reach a middle ground
  • After hours of debate, we finally found a middle ground.
  • It is difficult to reach a middle ground on this budget issue.
spanish: Aprecio tu perspectiva, así que busquemos un punto medio que aborde ambas preocupaciones.

text: I understand your frustration, let's focus on finding a {{c1::middle ground}} that works for both teams.
explanation: A 'middle ground' is a compromise or position of agreement between two opposing viewpoints or parties.
usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / compromise
  • Negotiations succeeded once both sides found a middle ground.
  • We need to establish a middle ground regarding work hours.
spanish: Entiendo su frustración, enfoquémonos en encontrar un punto medio/acuerdo que funcione para ambos equipos.

text: Let's schedule a call to {{c1::iron out}} these misunderstandings before the client meeting.
explanation: To 'iron out' is a phrasal verb meaning to resolve, smooth over, or eliminate minor problems or differences in views.
usage:
Pattern: iron out [problems / details / differences]
  • We need to iron out the details of the contract.
  • They met to iron out their political differences.
spanish: Agendemos una llamada para resolver/limar estas asperezas antes de la reunión con el cliente.

text: I want to make sure I fully grasp your point; could you please {{c1::elaborate on}} your concerns?
explanation: To 'elaborate on' means to add more details, explanation, or information to a statement that was previously brief.
usage:
Pattern: elaborate on [point / concerns / strategy]
  • Could you elaborate on how this database schema works?
  • The manager elaborated on the restructuring plan.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de entender completamente su punto; ¿podría por favor detallar/explayarse sobre sus preocupaciones?

text: Let's keep this discussion objective and avoid {{c1::pointing fingers}} at individuals.
explanation: To 'point fingers' is an idiomatic expression meaning to accuse or place blame on someone else for a failure or problem.
usage:
Pattern: point fingers / play the blame game
  • Instead of pointing fingers, let's focus on fixing the bug.
  • She is always quick to point fingers when things go wrong.
spanish: Mantengamos esta discusión objetiva y evitemos buscar culpables/echarle la culpa a individuos.

text: I appreciate you bringing this up; let's work together to {{c1::address the root cause}} of this issue.
explanation: To 'address the root cause' means to identify and fix the underlying, fundamental reason why a problem occurred, rather than just treating symptoms.
usage:
Pattern: address the root cause / resolve root issue
  • Rebooting the server only patches the symptom; we must address the root cause.
  • Our investigation aimed to address the root cause of the data breach.
spanish: Le agradezco que mencione esto; trabajemos juntos para abordar la causa raíz de este problema.

text: We seem to have reached a {{c1::deadlock}}; let's bring in a neutral third party to mediate.
explanation: A 'deadlock' (or impasse) is a situation in a negotiation where no progress is possible because neither party is willing to compromise.
usage:
Pattern: reach a deadlock / break a deadlock
  • The contract talks ended in a complete deadlock.
  • We need a mediator to help us break the deadlock.
spanish: Parece que hemos llegado a un punto muerto/callejón sin salida; involucremos a un tercero neutral para mediar.

text: I agree that we need to act fast, but we cannot afford to {{c1::cut corners}} on safety.
explanation: To 'cut corners' is an idiom meaning to do something in the easiest, cheapest, or fastest way, often sacrificing quality or safety standards.
usage:
Pattern: cut corners / skimp on quality
  • The builder cut corners and used cheap materials.
  • Never cut corners on database security audits.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo en que debemos actuar rápido, pero no podemos permitirnos tomar atajos/escatimar en seguridad.

text: Let's take a step back and look at the {{c1::bigger picture}} before making a decision.
explanation: The 'bigger picture' refers to the overall context, long-term goals, or broad view of a situation, rather than small details.
usage:
Pattern: look at the bigger picture / overview
  • Don't get bogged down in detail; look at the bigger picture.
  • A manager must always focus on the bigger picture.
spanish: Demos un paso atrás y miremos el panorama completo/la perspectiva general antes de tomar una decisión.

text: I apologize for the oversight; I will take full responsibility and {{c1::make it right}}.
explanation: To 'make it right' means to correct a mistake, repair a bad situation, or compensate someone for an error.
usage:
Pattern: make it right / repair the damage
  • We sent the client a refund to make it right.
  • If you make a mistake, do your best to make it right.
spanish: Pido disculpas por el descuido; asumiré toda la responsabilidad y lo corregiré/enmendaré.

text: We need to {{c1::double down on}} our core product line to capture market share this quarter.
explanation: double down on means to significantly strengthen your commitment, focus, or investment in a strategy, instead of spreading resources too thin.
usage:
Pattern: double down on [focus/strategy/effort]
  • We decided to double down on cloud security features.
  • The team doubled down on automated testing to fix regression bugs.
spanish: Necesitamos redoblar esfuerzos en nuestra línea de productos principal para capturar participación de mercado este trimestre.

text: Given our current budget parameters, we must {{c1::trim the fat}} in our operating expenses.
explanation: trim the fat is a common business idiom meaning to eliminate waste, unnecessary expenses, or redundancies to make operations leaner.
usage:
Pattern: trim the fat (idiom)
  • To achieve profitability, the division needs to trim the fat.
  • Trimming the fat in marketing helped us fund research and development.
spanish: Dados nuestros parámetros presupuestarios actuales, debemos recortar los gastos innecesarios en nuestros gastos operativos.

text: We need to align our department goals to support the company's {{c1::long-term roadmap}}.
explanation: A 'long-term roadmap' is a strategic plan that outlines the main goals and milestones of a company over a long period (e.g. 3 to 5 years).
usage:
Pattern: long-term roadmap / strategic vision
  • Our product roadmap spans over the next three years.
  • The director presented the long-term roadmap to the division.
spanish: Necesitamos alinear las metas de nuestro departamento para apoyar la hoja de ruta a largo plazo de la compañía.

text: My role is to steer the strategic direction and {{c1::empower our managers}} to execute it.
explanation: To 'empower managers' means giving them the authority, resources, and trust to make decisions and lead their teams independently.
usage:
Pattern: empower managers / delegate authority
  • Good directors empower managers rather than micromanage.
  • We must empower our front-line managers to resolve customer issues.
spanish: Mi rol es guiar la dirección estratégica y capacitar/dar autonomía a nuestros gerentes para ejecutarla.

text: We must ensure we are allocating our resources efficiently to {{c1::maximize ROI}}.
explanation: To 'maximize ROI' (Return on Investment) means to ensure that business spending yields the highest possible profit or strategic benefit.
usage:
Pattern: maximize ROI / boost returns
  • Our marketing budget is allocated to channels that maximize ROI.
  • The team restructured the project to maximize ROI.
spanish: Debemos asegurarnos de asignar nuestros recursos eficientemente para maximizar el retorno de la inversión.

text: I need to report our quarterly performance metrics directly to the {{c1::board of directors}}.
explanation: The 'board of directors' is the governing body of a corporation elected by shareholders to oversee management and protect their interests.
usage:
Pattern: board of directors / board members
  • The board of directors approved the acquisition.
  • He serves on the board of directors for three companies.
spanish: Necesito informar nuestras métricas de rendimiento trimestrales directamente a la junta directiva.

text: We are restructuring the department to improve cross-functional collaboration and {{c1::eliminate silos}}.
explanation: To 'eliminate silos' means to break down barriers between departments that prevent open communication, sharing of data, or collaborative work.
usage:
Pattern: eliminate silos / break down walls
  • A key goal of Agile is to eliminate silos between devs and QA.
  • We must eliminate departmental silos to speed up production.
spanish: Estamos reestructurando el departamento para mejorar la colaboración interfuncional y eliminar los silos/departamentos aislados.

text: We must foster a culture of innovation where calculated risk-taking is {{c1::encouraged rather than penalized}}.
explanation: This phrase describes a strategic environment where employees are motivated to try new ideas, knowing that mistakes in pursuit of innovation will not lead to negative career consequences.
usage:
Pattern: [something] is encouraged rather than penalized
  • Creative problem solving should be encouraged rather than penalized.
  • Taking initiative is encouraged rather than penalized in this team.
spanish: Debemos fomentar una cultura de innovación donde la toma de riesgos calculados sea alentada en lugar de penalizada.

text: I will represent our division at the upcoming global leadership summit to {{c1::showcase our achievements}}.
explanation: To 'showcase achievements' means to display or present positive results, milestones, or accomplishments to others in a prominent way.
usage:
Pattern: showcase achievements / highlight success
  • The annual report is an opportunity to showcase our achievements.
  • We built a dashboard to showcase team achievements to executives.
spanish: Representaré a nuestra división en la próxima cumbre global de liderazgo para exhibir/presentar nuestros logros.

text: We need to conduct a thorough risk assessment before entering this {{c1::joint venture}}.
explanation: A 'joint venture' is a business arrangement in which two or more parties agree to pool their resources for the purpose of accomplishing a specific task.
usage:
Pattern: joint venture / strategic partnership
  • The two auto giants formed a joint venture to build electric cars.
  • The project is a joint venture between public and private firms.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar una evaluación de riesgos exhaustiva antes de entrar en esta empresa conjunta/alianza comercial.

text: I want to ensure we are aligned, so I'd like to {{c1::address a pattern}} I've noticed in your recent deliverables.
explanation: address a pattern is a polite, constructive way to bring up a recurring issue in performance without making the employee feel immediately attacked.
usage:
Pattern: address a pattern of [behavior]
  • We need to address a pattern of late arrivals in morning standups.
  • Thank you for addressing a pattern that was affecting our release schedule.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que estemos alineados, por lo que me gustaría abordar un patrón que he notado en tus entregables recientes.

text: According to Kim Scott's Radical Candor framework, effective leadership requires balancing the two dimensions of {{c1::Care Personally}} (Cuidar Personalmente) and {{c2::Challenge Directly}} (Desafiar Directamente).
explanation: Failing to challenge directly while caring personally leads to 'Empatía Ruinosa' (Ruinous Empathy), which prevents growth. Challenging directly without caring leads to 'Agresión Ofensiva' (Obnoxious Aggression).
usage: Radical Candor is the 'sweet spot' where you care enough about someone to tell them the uncomfortable truth to help them grow.
spanish: Según el marco de Sinceridad Radical de Kim Scott, el liderazgo efectivo requiere equilibrar las dos dimensiones de Cuidar Personalmente y Desafiar Directamente.

text: During the 1-on-1 meeting, the manager noted: "While your code is robust, there is {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation. {{c2::Going forward}}, let's focus on adding README guides."
explanation: Room for improvement is a polite, constructive way to say that something needs development. **Going forward** is a transition phrase meaning 'in the future'.
usage:
Pattern: room for improvement in [area] / going forward, we should [action]
  • There is room for improvement in our test coverage.
  • Going forward, all code must be reviewed by two peers.
spanish: Durante la reunión 1 a 1, el gerente señaló: "Aunque tu código es robusto, hay margen de mejora en tu documentación. En el futuro, concentrémonos en agregar guías README".

text: I appreciate your hard work on this project; however, there is still {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation.
explanation: 'Room for improvement' is a polite, professional idiom meaning that something is not perfect and has potential to be better.
usage:
Pattern: room for improvement / areas of development
  • The report was good, but there's room for improvement.
  • There is always room for improvement in software performance.
spanish: Aprecio su arduo trabajo en este proyecto; sin embargo, todavía hay margen de mejora en su documentación.

text: To help you grow, I want to offer some {{c1::constructive feedback}} on your client presentation.
explanation: 'Constructive feedback' is supportive advice or critique designed to help someone improve their work or behavior, rather than simply pointing out faults.
usage:
Pattern: constructive feedback / constructive criticism
  • I always appreciate constructive feedback from my manager.
  • Try to phrase your comments as constructive feedback.
spanish: Para ayudarle a crecer, quiero ofrecerle retroalimentación constructiva sobre su presentación con el cliente.

text: You did a fantastic job managing the client's expectations; {{c1::keep up the good work}}!
explanation: 'Keep up the good work' is a common encouraging phrase used to praise someone's performance and motivate them to continue performing at a high level.
usage:
Pattern: keep up the good work / keep it up
  • The manager told us to keep up the good work.
  • Fantastic results this quarter, keep up the good work!
spanish: Hizo un trabajo fantástico gestionando las expectativas del cliente; ¡siga así (continúe con el buen trabajo)!

text: I've noticed that you've been missing deadlines; is there anything {{c1::holding you back}}?
explanation: To 'hold someone back' is a phrasal verb meaning to prevent someone from making progress, developing, or performing to their potential.
usage:
Pattern: hold [someone] back / block progress
  • What is holding you back from applying for the promotion?
  • Lack of funding is holding the research back.
spanish: He notado que ha estado perdiendo fechas límite; ¿hay algo que le impida avanzar/le esté frenando?

text: Let's set up a weekly check-in to track your progress and address any {{c1::pain points}} early.
explanation: A 'pain point' is a specific problem, obstacle, or difficulty experienced by employees, customers, or users.
usage:
Pattern: address pain points / resolve customer pain points
  • The new software addresses the main pain points of our team.
  • We need to identify user pain points in the checkout flow.
spanish: Establezcamos una reunión de control semanal para realizar un seguimiento de su progreso y abordar cualquier punto crítico/dificultad temprano.

text: Your technical skills are excellent, but you need to work on your {{c1::active listening}} during team meetings.
explanation: 'Active listening' is a communication technique that requires the listener to fully concentrate, understand, respond, and remember what is being said.
usage:
Pattern: practice active listening / active listening skills
  • Active listening is essential for effective conflict resolution.
  • She showed active listening by summarizing my points.
spanish: Sus habilidades técnicas son excelentes, pero necesita trabajar en su escucha activa durante las reuniones de equipo.

text: I want to recognize your effort; your dedication has made a {{c1::huge impact on}} the team's morale.
explanation: To 'make a huge impact on' means to have a very large, significant, or powerful effect or influence on a situation, group, or project.
usage:
Pattern: make a huge impact on [something]
  • The training made a huge impact on sales performance.
  • Your feedback has had a huge impact on my career growth.
spanish: Quiero reconocer su esfuerzo; su dedicación ha tenido un gran impacto en la moral del equipo.

text: When a client objects to a price increase, you can say: "I hear your {{c1::concerns regarding the cost}}; however, this new tier {{c2::delivers significant value by}} doubling your API rate limit."
explanation: Acknowledging the objection first shows respect, while 'delivers value by' steers the conversation toward benefits rather than expense.
usage:
Pattern: I hear your concerns regarding [topic]; however, this [solution] delivers value by [benefit]
  • I hear your concerns regarding the timeline; however, this phase delivers value by securing your data early.
  • We believe this update delivers significant value by reducing server load.
spanish: Cuando un cliente se opone a un aumento de precio, puedes decir: "Escucho sus inquietudes con respecto al costo; sin embargo, este nuevo nivel ofrece un valor significativo al duplicar su límite de velocidad de API".

text: If a user rejects a solution, offer options: "If that option doesn't {{c1::align with your objectives}}, we can {{c2::explore a phased roll-out}} instead."
explanation: Align with objectives means to match their goals. **Explore a phased roll-out** means to implement the solution in gradual steps rather than all at once.
usage:
Pattern: If [solution] doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore [alternative]
  • If the standard plan doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore a custom contract.
  • Let's explore a phased roll-out of the security policy.
spanish: Si un usuario rechaza una solución, ofrece opciones: "Si esa opción no se alinea con sus objetivos, podemos explorar una implementación gradual en su lugar".

text: To uncover the root of a client's hesitation: "What is your {{c1::primary reservation about}} moving forward with this integration?"
explanation: Primary reservation refers to the main doubt, worry, or objection holding someone back from making a decision.
usage:
Pattern: What is your primary reservation about [action]?
  • What is your primary reservation about migrating to the cloud?
  • His primary reservation about the project was the tight deadline.
spanish: Para descubrir la raíz de la vacilación de un cliente: "¿Cuál es su principal reserva sobre seguir adelante con esta integración?"

text: When a customer compares you to a competitor: "I understand that [Competitor] has {{c1::a lower price point}}, but our platform provides {{c2::enterprise-grade security}} that is not offered elsewhere."
explanation: Lower price point is a professional euphemism for 'cheaper'. **Enterprise-grade** denotes high-quality, robust features suited for large, demanding organizations.
usage:
Pattern: understand [competitor] has a lower price point, but our [product] provides [advantage]
  • I understand they have a lower price point, but our service provides 99.99% uptime.
  • We need enterprise-grade security to satisfy compliance requirements.
spanish: Cuando un cliente te compara con un competidor: "Entiendo que [Competidor] tiene un precio más bajo, pero nuestra plataforma ofrece seguridad de nivel empresarial que no se ofrece en otros lugares".

text: I understand your concern about the budget, but let me show you how this tool will {{c1::save you money}} in the long run.
explanation: To 'save money' means to reduce expenses or prevent financial waste, a key value proposition in professional sales.
usage:
Pattern: save money / cost savings
  • Moving to the cloud will save us money on infrastructure.
  • This energy-efficient device will save you money.
spanish: Entiendo su preocupación sobre el presupuesto, pero permítame mostrarle cómo esta herramienta le ahorrará dinero a largo plazo.

text: That's a valid point; however, our software integrates seamlessly with your {{c1::legacy systems}}.
explanation: A 'legacy system' is an old or outdated computer system, technology, or software application that a company continues to use because it is critical to their business.
usage:
Pattern: legacy systems / legacy technology
  • Replacing legacy systems can be extremely expensive and risky.
  • We built an API wrapper to connect our app to their legacy system.
spanish: Ese es un punto válido; sin embargo, nuestro software se integra a la perfección con sus sistemas heredados/antiguos.

text: To address your security concerns, we are fully compliant with all major {{c1::industry standards}}.
explanation: An 'industry standard' is a technical specification, requirement, or guideline widely accepted and followed in a specific field (e.g. ISO 27001, SOC 2).
usage:
Pattern: industry standards / compliance standards
  • Our security protocols meet the highest industry standards.
  • We must update our process to keep up with changing industry standards.
spanish: Para abordar sus preocupaciones de seguridad, cumplimos plenamente con todos los principales estándares de la industria.

text: I hear what you are saying, but our customer support team is available {{c1::around the clock}} to help you.
explanation: 'Around the clock' is an idiom meaning continuously, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
usage:
Pattern: around the clock / 24/7 support
  • The engineering team worked around the clock to fix the database.
  • We offer customer service around the clock.
spanish: Escucho lo que dice, pero nuestro equipo de soporte al cliente está disponible las 24 horas del día para ayudarle.

text: We can adjust the project scope to fit your timeline if that is the {{c1::deciding factor}} for you.
explanation: The 'deciding factor' is the key element, reason, or condition that makes a person resolve a choice or make a final decision.
usage:
Pattern: deciding factor / deal-breaker
  • Price was the deciding factor in our choice of vendor.
  • Safety features were the deciding factor when buying the car.
spanish: Podemos ajustar el alcance del proyecto para adaptarlo a su cronograma si ese es el factor decisivo para usted.

text: Let me walk you through our case studies to show how we solved similar problems for {{c1::other clients}}.
explanation: A 'case study' is a detailed analysis of a project or campaign, used as marketing evidence to show potential clients how a company successfully delivered value.
usage:
Pattern: case study / client success story
  • We published three new case studies on our website.
  • Our case studies prove that our algorithm improves conversion rates.
spanish: Permítame guiarle a través de nuestros estudios de caso para mostrar cómo resolvimos problemas similares para otros clientes.

text: Could you please {{c1::power cycle}} the router by unplugging it for 30 seconds?
explanation: power cycle is a technical verb meaning to turn an electronic device completely off (usually by cutting power) and back on again to reset it.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle [device] (e.g. power cycle the server, power cycle the modem)
  • If your connection drops, try power cycling the access point.
  • I power cycled the switch, which solved the IP conflict.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor apagar y encender el router desenchufándolo durante 30 segundos?

text: We are currently experiencing a {{c1::service disruption}} affecting our primary database, and our team is working to mitigate it.
explanation: service disruption is the formal corporate term for an outage or downtime. mitigate means to reduce the severity or impact of an ongoing incident.
usage:
Pattern: experience a service disruption | mitigate [incident/impact/risk]
  • The network migration caused a minor service disruption for 10 minutes.
  • We are deploying a hotfix to mitigate the database CPU spikes.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción del servicio que afecta a nuestra base de datos principal, y nuestro equipo está trabajando para mitigarlo.

text: The engineer reported: "We are experiencing a major {{c1::infrastructure outage}} due to a {{c2::database bottleneck}}, and we are preparing a root cause analysis."
explanation: Outage means service downtime. **Bottleneck** refers to a point of congestion in a system that slows down performance. **Root cause analysis (RCA)** is the diagnostic process to find the source of the issue.
usage:
Pattern: experience an outage due to [cause] / conduct a root cause analysis
  • The network outage was caused by a configuration error.
  • We conducted a root cause analysis to prevent future failures.
spanish: El ingeniero informó: "Estamos experimentando una interrupción importante de la infraestructura debido a un cuello de botella en la base de datos, y estamos preparando un análisis de causa raíz".

text: Have you tried turning the device off and on again to {{c1::power cycle}} it?
explanation: To 'power cycle' a device means to shut off the power to a hardware piece and then turn it back on, which clears memory and resets state.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle / reboot the router
  • IT support always asks you to power cycle the modem first.
  • Power cycling the switch resolved the packet loss.
spanish: ¿Ha intentado apagar y volver a encender el dispositivo para reiniciar el ciclo de energía?

text: I will open a support ticket for this issue, and our team will {{c1::look into it}} shortly.
explanation: To 'look into' is a common phrasal verb meaning to investigate, examine, or check the status of a problem or request.
usage:
Pattern: look into [issue / ticket / bug]
  • We are looking into the server latency report.
  • I'll look into why your payment was declined.
spanish: Abriré un ticket de soporte para este problema, y nuestro equipo lo revisará/investigará en breve.

text: Please make sure your security credentials are up to date to prevent login {{c1::lockouts}}.
explanation: A 'lockout' is a security state where a user is temporarily or permanently barred from accessing an account after too many failed password attempts.
usage:
Pattern: account lockout / lockout policy
  • Three failed attempts will trigger an automatic account lockout.
  • Contact IT support to resolve your password lockout.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que sus credenciales de seguridad estén actualizadas para evitar bloqueos de inicio de sesión.

text: We are currently experiencing a temporary server outage; we apologize for the {{c1::downtime}}.
explanation: 'Downtime' refers to the period during which a system, computer network, or server is unavailable or offline.
usage:
Pattern: server downtime / system downtime
  • Scheduled maintenance downtime will occur this Sunday.
  • Our service level agreement guarantees less than 0.01% downtime.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción temporal del servidor; nos disculpamos por el tiempo de inactividad.

text: Could you please send me a screenshot of the error message to help us {{c1::troubleshoot}} the problem?
explanation: To 'troubleshoot' means to identify, analyze, and resolve technical faults or bugs in a system by following a logical process of elimination.
usage:
Pattern: troubleshoot the problem / technical troubleshooting
  • We need to troubleshoot why the API is returning 500 errors.
  • He is very good at troubleshooting network connection problems.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviarme una captura de pantalla del mensaje de error para ayudarnos a diagnosticar/resolver el problema?

text: You need to update your operating system to the latest version to patch these {{c1::security vulnerabilities}}.
explanation: A 'security vulnerability' is a flaw or weakness in software or hardware that malicious actors can exploit to gain unauthorized access or execute code.
usage:
Pattern: patch security vulnerabilities / zero-day vulnerability
  • The patch fixes critical security vulnerabilities in the kernel.
  • Cybercriminals scan the web looking for unpatched security vulnerabilities.
spanish: Necesita actualizar su sistema operativo a la última versión para parchear estas vulnerabilidades de seguridad.

text: Please check your spam folder if you haven't received the password reset link {{c1::in your inbox}}.
explanation: An 'inbox' is the folder where newly received email messages are stored.
usage:
Pattern: in your inbox / email inbox
  • The confirmation code should arrive in your inbox in a minute.
  • My inbox is flooded with spam emails.
spanish: Por favor, revise su carpeta de correo no deseado (spam) si no ha recibido el enlace de restablecimiento de contraseña en su bandeja de entrada.

text: During the interview, she explained: "I {{c1::spearheaded the optimization}} of our CI/CD pipelines, which {{c2::streamlined our deployment cycle}} by 40%."
explanation: Spearhead is an active, high-impact verb meaning to lead an initiative. **Streamline** means to make a process more efficient by removing bottlenecks.
usage:
Pattern: spearhead the [action] of [system] / streamline [process]
  • He spearheaded the migration of our legacy systems.
  • We need to streamline our support ticketing process.
spanish: Durante la entrevista, ella explicó: "Lideré la optimización de nuestros pipelines de CI/CD, lo que simplificó nuestro ciclo de implementación en un 40%".

text: Could you walk me through your background and describe a project you are {{c1::particularly proud of}}?
explanation: To be 'particularly proud of' means to feel a deep sense of satisfaction or accomplishment regarding a specific work piece or result.
usage:
Pattern: particularly proud of [project / achievement]
  • I am particularly proud of the database migration we completed last month.
  • She is particularly proud of her team's growth.
spanish: ¿Podría guiarme a través de su trayectoria y describir un proyecto del cual esté especialmente orgulloso?

text: When faced with tight deadlines, how do you prioritize tasks and manage {{c1::under pressure}}?
explanation: To manage or work 'under pressure' means to maintain high performance, focus, and emotional control during high-stress conditions.
usage:
Pattern: work under pressure / perform under pressure
  • Our ideal candidate must be able to work under pressure.
  • I have learned to stay calm and organized under pressure.
spanish: Cuando se enfrenta a plazos de entrega ajustados, ¿cómo prioriza las tareas y se desenvuelve bajo presión?

text: I am looking for a role where I can apply my skills and grow {{c1::within the company}}.
explanation: To grow 'within the company' means to advance your career path, receive promotions, and take on new roles inside the same organization over time.
usage:
Pattern: grow within the company / internal promotion
  • There are many opportunities to grow within the company.
  • I want to build a long-term career and grow within the company.
spanish: Estoy buscando un puesto donde pueda aplicar mis habilidades y crecer dentro de la empresa.

text: Why are you interested in joining our team, and what do you know about our {{c1::core values}}?
explanation: 'Core values' are the fundamental beliefs and guiding principles that dictate behavior, decisions, and culture inside an organization.
usage:
Pattern: core values / company culture
  • Our core values include integrity, innovation, and teamwork.
  • Aligning with the company's core values is essential.
spanish: ¿Por qué está interesado en unirse a nuestro equipo, y qué sabe sobre nuestros valores fundamentales?

text: Could you give me an example of a time you had a conflict with a coworker and how you {{c1::resolved it}}?
explanation: To 'resolve a conflict' means to settle a disagreement, dispute, or friction between coworkers in a professional, constructive manner.
usage:
Pattern: resolve a conflict / settle a dispute
  • We resolved the conflict by scheduling a private discussion.
  • HR helps employees resolve conflicts in the office.
spanish: ¿Podría darme un ejemplo de alguna ocasión en la que haya tenido un conflicto con un compañero de trabajo y cómo lo resolvió?

text: I am comfortable working in both collaborative team environments and {{c1::independently}}.
explanation: To work 'independently' means to execute tasks, make decisions, and manage your time without constant supervision or direction.
usage:
Pattern: work independently / self-directed
  • This role requires someone who can work independently with minimal guidance.
  • He is capable of working independently on complex tasks.
spanish: Me siento cómodo trabajando tanto en entornos de equipo colaborativos como de manera independiente.

text: What are your salary expectations for this position, and do you have any {{c1::flexibility}}?
explanation: 'Salary expectations' refer to the target compensation range an applicant wants to earn in a new job. Asking about 'flexibility' queries if they are open to negotiation.
usage:
Pattern: salary expectations / target compensation
  • My salary expectations are in the range of $80,000 to $90,000.
  • We discussed salary expectations during the initial phone screening.
spanish: ¿Cuáles son sus expectativas salariales para este puesto, y tiene alguna flexibilidad?

text: Thank you for your time today; I look forward to hearing about the {{c1::next steps}} in the process.
explanation: The 'next steps' in a recruiting process refer to the subsequent phases (e.g. technical interview, reference checks, final offer).
usage:
Pattern: next steps / follow-up process
  • What are the next steps in your hiring process?
  • The recruiter will contact you regarding the next steps.
spanish: Gracias por su tiempo hoy; espero con interés saber de los siguientes pasos en el proceso.

text: Do you have any questions for us about the role or the team {{c1::dynamics}}?
explanation: 'Team dynamics' refer to the psychological forces and behavioral relationships that influence how a team communicates and collaborates.
usage:
Pattern: team dynamics / working relationships
  • I want to ask about the team dynamics and how tasks are assigned.
  • Resolving conflict is crucial to maintaining positive team dynamics.
spanish: ¿Tiene alguna pregunta para nosotros sobre el puesto o la dinámica del equipo?

text: I'd like you to {{c1::take the lead on}} this integration, but feel free to escalate if you hit any roadblocks.
explanation: take the lead on means to assume responsibility for directing or managing a specific project. escalate means to report a problem up the chain of command when tools or resources are blocked.
usage:
Patterns: take the lead on [project/task] | escalate to [someone]
  • She will take the lead on the customer migration.
  • If the vendor does not respond, escalate the ticket to me.
spanish: Me gustaría que lideres esta integración, pero no dudes en escalar si te encuentras con algún obstáculo.

text: It is my responsibility to delegate tasks and ensure my team has the support they need to {{c1::meet their targets}}.
explanation: To 'meet targets' means to achieve the specific goals, performance levels, or metrics set by company leadership.
usage:
Pattern: meet targets / achieve goals
  • The sales team worked hard to meet their targets this quarter.
  • We did not meet our performance targets last year.
spanish: Es mi responsabilidad delegar tareas y asegurar que mi equipo tenga el apoyo que necesita para cumplir con sus metas/objetivos.

text: We need to hold weekly one-on-one meetings to check in on progress and remove any {{c1::blockers}}.
explanation: A 'blocker' (or impediment) is an issue, bug, or dependency that prevents a team member from making progress on their tasks.
usage:
Pattern: remove blockers / clear roadblocks
  • My primary job as a manager is to remove blockers for my devs.
  • Do you have any blockers keeping you from finishing the script?
spanish: Necesitamos realizar reuniones individuales semanales para revisar el progreso y eliminar cualquier bloqueador/impedimento.

text: My goal is to support my team's professional development and help them {{c1::reach their full potential}}.
explanation: To 'reach your full potential' means to develop your talents, skills, and career capabilities to the maximum possible level.
usage:
Pattern: reach full potential / unlock potential
  • A good leader helps their employees reach their full potential.
  • Mentorship is key to helping junior devs reach their full potential.
spanish: Mi objetivo es apoyar el desarrollo profesional de mi equipo y ayudarles a alcanzar su máximo potencial.

text: We must manage our team's workload carefully to prevent burnout and maintain high {{c1::morale}}.
explanation: 'Morale' refers to the enthusiasm, confidence, and mental state of a group or team regarding their tasks and work environment.
usage:
Pattern: boost morale / low morale
  • Missing targets led to low morale in the sales department.
  • The team-building event was organized to boost morale.
spanish: Debemos gestionar el volumen de trabajo de nuestro equipo cuidadosamente para evitar el agotamiento y mantener la moral alta.

text: I need to coordinate with other department heads to align our resource allocation and {{c1::avoid duplication of effort}}.
explanation: To 'avoid duplication of effort' means to organize work so that two different people or teams do not spend resources doing the exact same task independently.
usage:
Pattern: avoid duplication of effort / streamline work
  • We merged the codebases to avoid duplication of effort.
  • Communication is essential to avoid duplication of effort in large projects.
spanish: Necesito coordinar con otros jefes de departamento para alinear nuestra asignación de recursos y evitar la duplicación de esfuerzos.

text: We will recognize outstanding performance by offering bonuses and public {{c1::praise}}.
explanation: 'Praise' refers to expressions of approval, commendation, or admiration. Offering 'public praise' honors someone's achievements in front of their peers.
usage:
Pattern: public praise / praise employees
  • Praise in public and criticize in private.
  • The CEO offered public praise to the migration team.
spanish: Reconoceremos el rendimiento sobresaliente ofreciendo bonos y elogios/reconocimiento público.

text: I need to review our team's performance data and present the findings to senior {{c1::stakeholders}}.
explanation: A 'stakeholder' is anyone who has an interest or investment in a business or project. 'Senior stakeholders' typically refer to directors, executives, or clients.
usage:
Pattern: senior stakeholders / key stakeholders
  • We must keep our senior stakeholders informed of project delays.
  • The requirements were defined by the key stakeholders.
spanish: Necesito revisar los datos de rendimiento de nuestro equipo y presentar los hallazgos a las partes interesadas/directivos sénior.

text: We must establish clear performance guidelines so everyone knows what is {{c1::expected of them}}.
